query: As of July 2026, what is the financial/bankruptcy status of Beyond Meat Inc? Has it filed for Chapter 11 or restructured debt? Any bankruptcy warnings, going concern doubts, debt restructuring, or imminent bankruptcy risk in 2025-2026?
answer:
I've completed my research on Beyond Meat's financial and bankruptcy status as of July 2026. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**No Chapter 11 filing.** Beyond Meat has NOT filed for bankruptcy and has repeatedly, explicitly denied any plans to do so.

**Major out-of-court debt restructuring (Oct 2025).** The company exchanged ~$1.12 billion (~97%) of its 0% Convertible Notes due 2027 for ~$208.7M of new 7.00% secured second-lien PIK Toggle Notes due 2030 plus ~316M new shares (~81% dilution). This qualified as a GAAP "troubled debt restructuring," generating a ~$548.7M non-cash gain that produced Beyond Meat's first-ever reported annual "profit" ($219.9M FY2025) — purely an accounting artifact. Total debt dropped from ~$1.2B to ~$415.7M.

**No going-concern qualification — but material weaknesses.** The FY2025 10-K (filed April 9, 2026) received an unqualified opinion from Deloitte with NO going-concern language, because ~$208–217M cash vs. ~$145M annual burn cleared the 12-month test. However, Deloitte issued an **adverse opinion on internal controls** due to material weaknesses (inventory accounting), and the 10-K was delayed multiple times.

**Fragile liquidity.** Year-end 2025: $217.5M cash vs. $415.7M debt; FY2025 burn $144.9M (+47% YoY). A $15M minimum liquidity covenant is a live constraint. Also drew a $100M Ahimsa-affiliated term loan at 12% PIK.

**Nasdaq delisting risk.** Deficiency letter issued March 4, 2026; **deadline August 31, 2026** to close ≥$1 for 10 consecutive days; reverse-split authority obtained. Stock trades ~$0.66 (down ~91% from 52-week high, >99% from 2019 peak).

**Bankruptcy warnings.** Creditsafe, TheStreet, and analysts repeatedly flagged Chapter 11 risk throughout 2025–2026; the company denies it. Risk is elevated and structurally fragile (negative FCF, declining revenue, delisting risk), but not imminent given the extended runway.

The full structured findings, sources, and data points have been recorded.

sources.title: Beyond Meat Posts First Annual Profit—But Only After a $549 Million Debt-Restructuring Gain
sources.snippet: $219.9M FY2025 net income; $548.7M non-cash troubled-debt-restructuring gain; year-end total debt $415.7M, cash $217.5M; operating cash use $144.9M; $100M Unprocessed Foods term loan at 12% PIK.

sources.title: Will BYND's FY2025 10-K include going concern language? (Resolved NO)
sources.snippet: FY2025 10-K filed 2026-04-09; Deloitte unqualified opinion, no going-concern language; adverse ICFR opinion due to material weaknesses; ~$208.2M cash vs $144.9M burn.

sources.title: Beyond Meat's Existential Reset: Can Margin Repair Outrun Category Collapse?
sources.snippet: Q1 2026: $411.6M remaining debt, $11.8M cash burn, $15M minimum liquidity covenant, gross margin +3.4% vs -10.1% YoY, volumes -19.5%.

sources.title: Beyond Meat faces new financial woes, Chapter 11 bankruptcy risk
sources.snippet: InvestingPro Financial Health Score 1.03 (WEAK); material weakness disclosure; ~$131.1M cash at Sept 27, 2025 vs ~$1.2B debt pre-exchange; company denies bankruptcy.

sources.title: Beyond Meat taking longer to pay its bills as plant-based sales plunge
sources.snippet: Creditsafe DBT rose from 8 days (Aug 2024) to 19 days (Jul 2025); cash $103M at June 28, 2025; company says not planning to file bankruptcy.

sources.title: Beyond Meat on the Verge of Financial Ruin
sources.snippet: Q2 2025 revenue down ~20%; DBT peaked at 20 (May 2025); Creditsafe cites Chapter 11 as possible reset; liquidity into 2027 per Seeking Alpha.

sources.title: Beyond Meat 8-K — Nasdaq $1 bid-price warning and reverse split option (March 4, 2026)
sources.snippet: Nasdaq deficiency letter March 4, 2026; company considering reverse stock split.

sources.title: Is a Beyond Meat Turnaround Still Possible in 2026?
sources.snippet: Delisting notice from Nasdaq after 30 days below $1; deadline Aug 31, 2026 to regain $1 for 10 consecutive days.

sources.title: Why Beyond Meat Stock Is Down — Jefferies cuts PT to $0.70
sources.snippet: Q4 2025 revenue -19.7% YoY to $61.6M; gross margin 2.3%; net income from one-time non-cash debt-restructuring gain; material weaknesses; EBITDA-positive target late 2026.

key_facts: Beyond Meat has NOT filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as of July 2026; the company repeatedly and explicitly denied bankruptcy plans.

In October 2025, Beyond Meat completed a major OUT-OF-COURT debt restructuring, exchanging ~$1.12 billion (~97%) of its 0% Convertible Notes due 2027 for up to ~$202.5M of new 7.00% Convertible Senior Secured Second Lien PIK Toggle Notes due 2030 plus ~316-318M new shares (~81% of the post-exchange company).

The restructuring qualified as a GAAP 'troubled debt restructuring,' generating a ~$548.7M non-cash accounting gain that produced Beyond Meat's first-ever reported annual profit of $219.9M for FY2025 (vs. a $160.3M loss in 2024) — an accounting artifact, not operating profit.

The FY2025 10-K (filed April 9, 2026) did NOT include going-concern/substantial-doubt language; Deloitte & Touche issued an unqualified opinion on the financials but an ADVERSE opinion on internal control (material weaknesses, notably inventory accounting).
Total debt was reduced from ~$1.2 billion to ~$415.7 million carrying value at year-end 2025; cash & equivalents (incl. restricted) were ~$217.5 million.
FY2025 operating cash burn was $144.9 million (up 47% from $98.8M in 2024); Q1 2026 cash burn slowed to ~$11.8M (lowest in two years).

Beyond Meat secured a $100M senior secured delayed-draw term loan from Unprocessed Foods LLC (Ahimsa Foundation affiliate) at 12% PIK interest (rising to 17.5% after Feb 2030), plus ~$149M via ATM equity sales.

Nasdaq issued a delisting deficiency letter on March 4, 2026 (stock below $1 for 30 consecutive days); compliance deadline is August 31, 2026, and the company obtained reverse-split authority (not yet executed as of latest data).
The 2030 notes/term loan carry a $15M minimum liquidity covenant, a live constraint on the company.
Creditsafe flagged Beyond Meat's Days Beyond Terms rising from ~4-8 days (late 2024) to ~19-20 days (mid-2025) vs. ~12 industry average, and repeatedly cited Chapter 11 bankruptcy risk.
BYND stock traded ~$0.656 (~July 10-13, 2026), market cap ~$338M, down ~91% from its 52-week high of $7.69 and >99% from its 2019 peak (~$235).
Q1 2026 (ended March 31, 2026): revenue $58.2M, net loss $28.5M; gross margin improved to +3.4% YoY from -10.1%; volumes still down ~19.5% YoY.

data_points.metric: FY2025 net cash used in operating activities (burn)
